collections/Counter/elements.name: elements
collections/Counter/elements.description: Return an iterator over elements repeating each as many times as its count. Elements are returned in the order first encountered. If an element’s count is less than one, elements() will ignore it. >>> c = Counter(a=4, b=2, c=0, d=-2) >>> sorted(c.elements()) ['a', 'a', 'a', 'a', 'b', 'b']

info.excerpt: Return an iterator over elements repeating each as many times as its count. Elements are returned in the order first encountered. If an element’s count is less than one, elements() will ignore it. >>>...

collections/deque/extend.name: extend
collections/deque/extend.description: Extend the right side of the deque by appending elements from the iterable argument.

info.excerpt: Extend the right side of the deque by appending elements from the iterable argument.

collections/deque/extendleft.name: extendleft
collections/deque/extendleft.description: Extend the left side of the deque by appending elements from iterable. Note, the series of left appends results in reversing the order of elements in the iterable argument.

info.excerpt: Extend the left side of the deque by appending elements from iterable. Note, the series of left appends results in reversing the order of elements in the iterable argument.
